title: Tragacantha capillipes
intro:
Unfortunately, I couldn't find any information about Tragacantha capillipes. However, I can provide information about Tragacanth (also known as Tragacantha or Astragalus gummifer), which might be a different species or a common name for Tragacantha capillipes.

Tragacanth is a type of gum-resin obtained from the stems and leaves of certain species of Astragalus, a genus of legumes. The health benefits of Tragacanth include its potential to act as a laxative, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant, making it useful in treating various gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include tragacanthic acid, arabic gum, and other polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Tragacanth is commonly used in herbal preparations such as powders, capsules, and topical creams, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine to treat conditions like diarrhea, constipation, and wounds.
taxonomy: Tragacantha capillipes is a member of the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or legume family. It is a type of shrub or small tree found in tropical regions. This plant is also known as the 'thorny acacia', and its classification is further divided into the genus Acacia, which is a part of the subfamily Mimosoideae. The genus Acacia is then classified into the family Fabaceae. Tragacantha capillipes is also sometimes classified under the genus Vachellia.
